issue_title: Memory usage with conda installation (on Travis)
issue_body:
Dear all,

We are using Travis for continuous integration. Our code base depends on a number of Python and R/Bioconductor packages that I am installing with Conda.

As you may know, Travis builds for open-source projects come with resource restrictions:
http://docs.travis-ci.com/user/ci-environment/#The-Build-Machine

namely, 3GB RAM + 1.5GB swap with 2 cores.

My problem here is with RAM usage. Our Travis builds fail due to the target system running out of memory and after some troubleshooting I have realised that the command:
```
conda create -n env pkg1 pkg2 etc.
```

consumes most of the RAM but in a non-deterministic fashion.

In a failed Travis build the amount of RAM, as reported by `free`, goes from 1.2GB to 4.5GB (maximum allowed by Travis). Please see before `conda create`:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262464#L124

and after:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262464#L571

This is the error reported by Travis:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262464#L682

In a successful Travis build the amount of RAM goes from 1.2GB to 1.8GB. Please see before `conda create`:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262465#L124

and after:
https://travis-ci.org/CGATOxford/cgat/jobs/54262465#L571

I have also found this thread:
https://github.com/travis-ci/travis-ci/issues/2904

kindly asking for higher amount of resources for Travis builds but that is not going to happen soon.

I have used `conda clean --index-cache` with no success. I was wondering whether you could provide advice on this or do you think I need to ask to Travis forum instead?

PS: My Conda recipes for R/Bioconductor packages are not uploaded to Git Hub yet but I am willing to do so soon, just in case anyone is interested in having them.

issue_title: Is it possible to get the package URL for download?
issue_body: My work PC is behind a firewall with rather limited network connection, so it's usually hard to install package with `conda install`.  So my question is whether is it possible to get the package URL from conda, then download the package on another place？

issue_title: Add local observation count to variant list page frequency pop-up
Increasing timeout flag for scout and scout stage
issue_body:
We are having an issue while visualizing case reports for some samples in scout. The case report is not produced and we get a 502 Bad Gateway error ([CRITICAL] WORKER TIMEOUT).

I think it might be worth trying to increase the Gunicorn timeout flag a bit (now it only is 60) to see if this fixes the problem, before thinking about more invasive ways..

Could we try @ingkebil ?

issue_title: Full header not returned in AlignmentFile text() method for specific bam file
difference between pileups and nsegments
issue_body:
Hi, I use pysam to get reads aligned to a certain base,  the code is as following:

import pysam 
samfile = pysam.AlignmentFile(test.bam)
for pileup_column in samfile.pileup(region='1:115256530-115256530', max_depth=200000):
      print(pileup_column.nsegments)
      print(len(pileup_column.pileups)

the result is nsegments is 11948 but len(pileups) is 5981, so  I wonder why reads are missing in pileups

issue_title: [Enhancement] Add option to synchronize navigation pane to editor
issue_body:
Just as the lock button was added to the file system pane I suggest to add a synch button to the navigation pane.
If active the navigation pane would highlight (and position to) the entry for the section, paragraph, ... which is active in the editor view. Scrolling in the editor view will therefore lead to a change in the navigation pane when another section, paragraph, ... is edited.

issue_title: Add lacp_mode keyword argument to Port-Channel set_members method
issue_body:
Right now the set_members method only takes a list of members.  Inside the method it tries to look at existing members to determine the lacp_mode.  This doesn't work when adding the first members to the channel-group.  This enhancement would add a keyword arg that would supercede the lacp_mode present on other members (if found).

Related to https://github.com/arista-eosplus/ansible-eos/issues/36

issue_title: Failing to break lease for PREMIUM_LRS stored Blobs
issue_body: I have several virtual machines which uses a bunch of blobs. these blobs are divided into two storages (PREMIUM_LRS and STANDARD_LRS). If i try to restore a blob, i shutdown the virutel machines which is using the blob and then break the lease (instead of deleting the VM). this works fine for STANDARD_LRS stored blobs. issue_title: pretty-format-json: allow specifying tab indentation
issue_body:
[`pretty-format-json` has an integer `indent` parameter](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks/blob/6b1aa19425b35f8c34f43ada8f1045b028dc19a5/pre_commit_hooks/pretty_format_json.py#L37) that specifies the number of spaces to use (i.e., indentation width). This is an enhancement proposal, with the aim that tabs can be specified as indentation delimiter.

[Since CPython 3.2](https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/json.html#basic-usage) one can use strings instead of integers as `indent` argument. The main use for that is to specify tabs as indentation. This has some advantages and proponents ([1](http://lea.verou.me/2012/01/why-tabs-are-clearly-superior/), [2](http://nickgravgaard.com/elastic-tabstops/)). For JSON, vertical alignment makes less sense than for programming language source code (e.g., long lists of parameter names), so a TAB-only indentation is feasible and advantageous, since it compresses the data (useful for webdev) and allows for configurable indentation width (useful for hard-to-read JSON).

This seems easy to implement by me or you, I'm just looking for feedback on this.

To reliably get all intersection points, one needs to sweep along all 6 axis directions with some super small distance > 0 which of course is super expensive since it requires 6 sweep tests.

issue_title: Disable Cookie Handling
issue_body:
I'd like to disable cookie processing in the general case for the session but to allow them to be set for requests with redirects.  For example, a request to `httpbin.org/cookies/set?a=1` results in a redirect to `httpbin.org/cookies` with the appropriate `Set-Cookie` header.  If I use a custom cookie jar (per Issue #957) that implements `update_cookies` as a no-op, when the redirect is followed, the cookies set in upstream requests aren't applied during subsequent redirects.

Aside from disabling redirects, setting cookies manually in the case of a redirect and following it manually, is there a way to deal with cookies more granularly than on a per-session basis?

issue_title: Feature request: education mode that supress all automatic outputs
issue_body: The request: add a possibility to turn on special "education mode" that disables automatic printing of the last expression in the cell. ### Rationale I do a lot of teaching using Jupyter. It is great environment for education. However, one feature, which is very useful for production use, is really bad in education: automatic output printing. At the very beginning of Python lessons we study data types, variables and so on. So it is natural to do something like. ``` [In]: x = 5 y = x + 1 [In]: y [Out]: 6 ``` We also study `print` function and do something like ``` [In]: x = 5 y = x + 1 print("x =", x) print("y =", y) x = 5 y = 6 ``` After that, we usually study functions, and here our problems begin. Consider the following example. ``` [In] def print_xsquare(x): print(x ** 2) def return_xsquare(x): return x ** 2 [In] print_xsquare(4) 16 [In] return_xsquare(4) [Out]: 16 ``` The students become confused: there is a lot of misundestanding on the difference between these two examples. As the visible outputs of functions `print_xsquare` and `return_xsquare` are so similar in Jupyter (you have to notice small `[Out]` word to get the difference), and the concept of function is new to most of my students, they tend to believe that `print` and `return` are somewhat similar operations. And it demands lot of efforts to explain the difference. It happens every single time we teach functions. So I finally come to conclusion that it is better to temporary turn off automatic outputting in Jupyter at the beginning of the the course and activate it only when functions, return values, values of expressions and so on are well understood. So I'm asking for this feature and I belive it will be useful for all of us, who teach Python to novices in software programming with Jupyter.

issue_title: executing a code block at end of document hides output
issue_body:
Whenever I execute a code block at the end of a document the existing output is cleared causing the document to scroll, when the computation is complete the output is then displayed but at this point it appears off the bottom of the page.

To work around this I tend to create ~20 empty blocks after my last "real" code block, such that when I run code it's output doesn't disappear.  I was wondering whether you would consider it bad form to add a temporary (blank) `div` to the end of the document (of the same height as the output that's being removed) and then remove it when the code has finished running.

This does seem a bit of a hack, maybe setting a `min-height` on the output to keep it the same until execution has finished?  Happy to submit a patch if other's think this is also a problem.

issue_title: (feature request) add standalone input and output consoles/cells for rapid development
issue_body:
For development purposes, it would be nice if there were an option for input and output cells that existed _outside of the notebook_, and (as an option) always on-screen.  This is a common feature available in many dev. environments.

Why I'm requesting:
a) easier to test/debug small code sections before committing/integrating into current script/notebook
b) for long cells, an easy way to avoid scrolling up and down to compare input and output.

I'd also note that this would not interfere with the current jupyter notebook design; simply closing/hiding/disabling the "console cells" would leave the notebook unchanged.

I found this is controlled in notebook `ContentsManager.new_untitled()`, which validates content type, and the file extension is always '.ipynb' or '.txt'

How can I override the behavior, with notebook extension? I don't want to touch notebook code.
so that I could create new file with different file extension like '.xls', '.cal' etc ...

thanks!

issue_title: Add a "hide code button"
issue_body: I think it'd be great to have an option of hiding all code cells, while leaving behind markdown and plots. That way you can make a smooth flowing presentation without any long code between text/images, but if a person wants to see the code that generated your plots they can just unhide it.

issue_title: Zarr chunked storage uses way too small chunks
issue_body: Currently Zarr is being left to guess chunks given only an initial portion of the data when using the chunked module, resulting in tiny chunk sizes.

issue_title: Ability to inspect the primitive type used to store data
issue_body:
As a developer using schematics to automatically convert a model to an open schema format (e.g. json-schema), it would be very useful to be able to inspect the python data type used to store the primitive data (i.e. one of `str`, `int`, `float`, `bool`, `dict`, or `list`).  I propose a class attribute named `PRIMITIVE_TYPE`.  This is not something that would be required for a custom model to implement in order operate correctly, but it would be great for the built-in models to set the standard for users to follow.  

It can also be used to create variation through inheritance, as in:

``` python
class NumberType(BaseType):
    PRIMITIVE_TYPE = None
    ...

class IntType(NumberType):
    PRIMITIVE_TYPE = int
    ...

class FloatType(NumberType):
    PRIMITIVE_TYPE = float
    ...
```

issue_title: Key name should not have space in it
issue_body:
SafeNet is a popular KMIP server.  However, it does not allow any spaces in the name of the key.  Therefore, the create script should not use "Test Key" as name in order to support SafeNet.  The patch below works on SafeNet 8.1.0:

diff --git a/kmip/demos/units/create.py b/kmip/demos/units/create.py
index 8a08cc1..22d6f95 100644
--- a/kmip/demos/units/create.py
+++ b/kmip/demos/units/create.py
@@ -107,7 +107,7 @@ if __name__ == '__main__':
     length_obj = attribute_factory.create_attribute(attribute_type,
                                                     length)
     name = Attribute.AttributeName('Name')
-    name_value = Name.NameValue('Test Key')
+    name_value = Name.NameValue('TestKey')
     name_type = Name.NameType(NameType.UNINTERPRETED_TEXT_STRING)
     value = Name(name_value=name_value, name_type=name_type)
     name = Attribute(attribute_name=name, attribute_value=value)

issue_title: Picard MarkDuplicates denies the existence of the SAM 0x800 flag
issue_body:
I give up: https://sourceforge.net/p/samtools/mailman/message/32800679/. Supposedly this was fixed in Picard 1.96? That would make sense, if we weren't running:

```
$ docker run quay.io/ucsc_cgl/picardtools:1.95--dd5ac549b95eb3e5d166a5e310417ef13651994e --version
picard-tools
Copyright 2010 The Broad Institute
```

?????????
\<pained facial expression\>
More sleuthing:

```
$  apt-get changelog picard-tools
picard-tools (1.105-1) unstable; urgency=low

  * New upstream release 1.105
  * Conforms with Policy 3.9.5.

 -- Olivier Sallou <osallou@debian.org>  Tue, 24 Dec 2013 11:16:18 +0100
```

Which checks out when cross referenced with the Picard releases page.

[Anyways, as far as I can tell, the fix isn't to—you know—fix Picard](https://www.biostars.org/p/75693/), but rather, to run `bwa mem` with the `-M` flag ("Mark shorter split hits as secondary (for Picard compatibility).") because the correct way to fix a trivial bug is to introduce intentionally incorrect behavior into another tool.

\[<another pained expression\>](https://www.biostars.org/p/176814/)

Anyways, it's not clear if we can pass the `-M` flag via bwakit. I'm not much of a Perl person, but AFAIK, there's no way to [add to the bwa mem options here](https://github.com/lh3/bwa/blob/master/bwakit/run-bwamem#L146).

So, TBD.

issue_title: currency filter fails if there's no active translation
issue_body:
```python
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'find'
```

This is because Django 1.7 used to return LANGUAGE_CODE if there's no active translation. Modify line 25 in `templatetags.currency_filters` to restore old behavior:
```python
        'locale': to_locale(get_language() or settings.LANGUAGE_CODE),
```

issue_title: Hosting Bundle installer removes RC version of 2.1.0, breaking all RC apps
issue_body:
# Issue Title

The Hosting Bundle Installer of .NET Core 2.1.0 removes the 2.1.0-rc1 version of the ASP.NET Core shared frameworks (shared framework M.NetCore.App 2.1.0-rc1 is still left), thus breaking every ASP.NET Core 2.1.0-rc1 app on the system since there is no roll-forward from RC to RTM.

# Steps to reproduce

(EDIT) Repro system: Windows Server 2016, no prior .NET Core installations

1. Install hosting bundle installer for 2.1.0-rc1
2. Deploy 2.1.0-rc1 apps to the system (e.g. using `Microsoft.AspNetCore.All` sharedfx)
3. Install hosting bundle installer for 2.1.0 RTM
4. Observe that the 2.1.0-rc1 apps break and are no longer able to run.

# Expected behavior

The RTM hosting bundle shall not uninstall previous version of the shared framework for which there is no roll-forward logic in hostfxr.

cc @leecow @natemcmaster (don't know whom to /cc)

issue_title: Renewer needs to reload webserver.
issue_body:
Hi, 
I just noticed my certs had renewed successfully, but the old certs were still showing on the website. This was not a big deal immediately, but after 29 days they would have expired, and that would cause problems. 
So, for a fully automated solution, the letsencrypt renew command should reload the webserver config. In my case this was a matter of 'service nginx reload' and the new certs appeared. This command should only be run on a successful renewal. i.e. when the cert has less than 30 days to run. 
Perhaps this could be addressed by a line in the relevant /etc/letsencrypt/renew/domain.conf file. Something like
run_on_successful_renewal = 'service nginx reload'

issue_title: Raise a better exception if context is not available for Function
issue_body:
Currently, if the function passed to a `Function` field needs context and the Schema's context is `None`, the error is stored as a validation error.

```python
class MySchema(Schema):
    foo = fields.Function(lambda obj, ctx: ctx['bar'].lower())

sch = MySchema()
sch.context = None
data, errs = sch.dump({})  # should raise an error
```

Instead, an error should be raised. Perhaps a `NoContextError`.

issue_title: missing and default values should be specified in deserialized form
issue_body:
Marshmallow expects `missing` and `default` values to be specified in a pre-serialized form, which is inconvenient and unintuitive.

Here is a schema which I would expect to work:

``` python
import datetime
import uuid

class TestSchema(Schema):
    id = fields.UUID(
        missing=uuid.uuid1)
    ts = fields.DateTime(
        format='rfc',
        missing=datetime.datetime.now)
```

However, it does not:

``` python
schema = TestSchema()
pprint.pprint(tuple(schema.load({})))
({}, {'id': [u'Not a valid UUID.'], 'ts': [u'Not a valid datetime.']})
```

Instead, I have to convert my `default` and `missing` values to primitive types, which undermines the purpose of a library like marshmallow that already knows how to do this.  For data types like datetimes, which have many possible serialized formats, I have to keep the formatting of my `default` and `missing` values in sync with my field properties, which is fragile and convoluted:

``` python
from email.Utils import formatdate

class TestSchema(Schema):
    id = fields.UUID(
        missing=lambda: str(uuid.uuid1()))
    ts = fields.DateTime(
        format='rfc',
        missing=formatdate)
```

In the case of `default` values, marshmallow does not attempt to validate them, so no error is raised, but the resulting document is not deserializable.

issue_title: Remove ipc module
Detecting fixed points to reduce execution time
issue_body:
## Description

Investigate wither using dominators in CFG and detecting a lack of change in a dominator basic block can profitably reduce unecceary searching. 

## Background

A [dominator tree](https://help.eclipse.org/photon/index.jsp?topic=%2Forg.eclipse.mat.ui.help%2Fconcepts%2Fdominatortree.html) has the property that the basic block acts as a choke point for each of the basic blocks in its decendents. Therefrore if in tracing there is no change in a dominiator, there will be no change in the children and so they don't have to be scanned. 

It could be that subsequently there's a change in one of a basic-block's ancestor dominators, and then the basic block would again be scanned.

issue_title: fp.rem (-0) (0.5) should be -0, but Z3 produces +0
issue_body: Happy to provide an SMTLib benchmark if that'd help; but the subject says it all. The semantics of fp.rem is such that the output should follow the sign of the first argument, but Z3 is violating that in this particular case. Note that this is similar to (but not the same as) #80.

issue_title: Should theming be overriding base.html the way it does?
issue_body:
I spent an hour today working out why several Django 1.8 changes weren't showing up.

I'd used 'theming' to fix some CSS and it overrides base.html. So we need to update the included base.html to match the Django version

This means maintaining a different base.html for each Django version and figure out how to load the correct one for a given install.

Considering the only reasons for overriding it are to add a single tag loader and a tag that loads some CSS it strikes me that this is too high a price to pay.

There's also the problem that only one app can override base.html - if another app that tries the same technique then it's down to the ordering of INSTALLED_APPS

This can be partially solved with documentation - tell people what to put in their base.html if they need to - but it gets messy.

I wonder if this could be improved in Django core. I'm still mulling it over but I thought I'd start a conversation.

issue_title: TensorBoard plots switch to scientific notation on the y axis too early, which makes plots harder to read.
issue_body:
TensorBoard plots switch to scientific notation for 5 digit numbers, which requires more characters to represent, and actually hides relevant precision, as demonstrated in the attached screenshot.

![screen shot 2018-05-22 at 11 27 56 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12631059/40382403-36a3c312-5db3-11e8-942a-e680c5c463d3.png)

-1.329e+4 -- 9 characters for 4 digits of precision and sign.
-13290 -- 6 characters for 5 digits of precision and sign.

It seems clear that scientific notation should only be enabled when the range of the plots contains numbers with more digits than would be used with the (less precise) scientific notation representation. In other words, scientific notation should only be used when one or both ends of the range has a magnitude >= 10^(5+desired digits of precision), where the 5 comes from the first digit, the decimal point, and the minimum number of exponent characters, which is 3.

The same basic reasoning also applies to numbers close to 0.

 - TensorBoard version (from pip package, also printed out when running `tensorboard`)
TensorBoard 67
 - Python version (e.g. 2.7, 3.5)
Python 2.7

issue_title: wrong panid used in discovery request.
issue_body:
It looks like the factoryreset, resets the radio (via otPlatReset) but does not reset the panid in the openthread stack.
According to the documentation the factoryreset will “Delete all stored settings, and signal a platform reset.”
Shouldn’t the panid in the openthread stack then also not be set to 0xffff ?

This can result in the wrong panId is used during Joining.
This can be seen during e.g. during certification test 8.1.1
In my test, the DUT is controlled by the official Thread test harness (via the THCI).
The test harness performs several settings on the DUT before the test.
The settings before the joining starts are:
1) (… many other settings…)
2) factoryreset
3) commissioner provisioningurl
4) extaddr 166e0a0000000001
5) channel 20
6) dataset channel 20
7) ifconfig up
8) joiner start threadjpaketest
… at this point the discovery starts.

As you can see in the settings above, no new panId was set _AFTER_ the factoryreset (note: it is set several times to 0xface _BEFORE_ the factoryreset).

But in the sniffer log the discovery request is sent with destination panid 0xface, and no source panid. (Therefor the source panid is also 0xface)
However, given the factoryreset, the source and destination panids should have been 0xffff.

issue_title: Allow different `MAIL FROM` or `Return-Path` and `From` headers.
issue_body:
Thanks for writing this great library!

I'm using Anymail 1.3, python-sparkpost 1.3.5, and Django 2.0.2. 

I've also raised this issue on https://github.com/SparkPost/python-sparkpost/issues/161 but I'm starting to think this issue is better suited to Django-Anymail.

The issue is in trying to create a bounce domain for emails. [Django's solution](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/9214#no1) can be seen [here in the codebase](https://github.com/django/django/blob/a38ae914d89809aed6d79337b74a8b31b6d3849a/django/core/mail/message.py#L256). 

To create a custom bounce domain, one puts the bounce domain in the `from_email` parameter, and a custom `From` header for the message's `From` address. This way you can send an email with a custom `Return-Path` header like mail@bounces.example.com and have the message's `From` header be something else like inbox@example.com. 

Because this is a Django library, my suggestion is to follow Django's standard in checking for a custom `From` header before setting the `from_email`. This way one can switch to/from vanilla Django to Django-Anymail without having to rewrite EmailMultiAlternatives.

Happy to create a PR if this makes sense/would be helpful?

issue_title: Get common name from CSR in new_order in ClientV2
issue_body:
https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/5578#issue-169507815.

We have code in `certbot.crypto_util` that does this with the help of some ACME code. The code in `new_order` for extracting SANs is nicer than the code in `acme.crypto_util._pyopenssl_cert_or_req_san`. What I'd do is make (a) function(s) in `acme.crypto_util` that takes a string CSR and uses the SANs extracting code from `new_order` with the common name extracting code from `certbot.crypto_util` and returns the list of domains. If we're currently removing duplicates, we should do this here as well.

issue_title: certbot-auto is not cleaning up temporary directory

The certbot-auto (letsencrypt-auto) script leaves behind a temporary directory every time it runs.  This looks like a regression as of release v0.5.0 (commit 4d4cfb414f2847f1814cfe2949c6e76f112e4ade) which removes the `trap "rm -rf '$TEMP_DIR'" EXIT` statement.

Assuming removing the `trap` was intentional (it may not have been; I can't find an issue or commit that refers specifically to that change, and it also temporarily disappeared in the "bad" pip8 branch merge), here's where the problem is:

```sh
if [ "$NO_SELF_UPGRADE" != 1 ]; then
  echo "Checking for new version..."
  TEMP_DIR=$(TempDir)

  ...

  if ! REMOTE_VERSION=`"$LE_PYTHON" "$TEMP_DIR/fetch.py" --latest-version` ; then
    echo "WARNING: unable to check for updates."
  elif [ "$LE_AUTO_VERSION" != "$REMOTE_VERSION" ]; then

    ...

    # TODO: Clean up temp dir safely, even if it has quotes in its path.
    rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
  fi  # A newer version is available.

  >>>$TEMP_DIR cleanup should be here instead<<<
fi  # Self-upgrading is allowed.
```

issue_title: Add support in certbot/dns-route53 for CNAMEed zones
issue_body:
## Introduction
When only hosting a sub-domain as zone on AWS Route53, e.g. auth.example.net, this could be used for DNS-01 challenges, when adding an appropriate records like:
```
; ACME authentication sub-domain hosted as ZONE on Amazon's Route 53

auth            IN      NS              ns-1234.awsdns-01.org.
auth            IN      NS              ns-768.awsdns-02.net.

; Redirection via CNAME to Route 53 managed auth-zine

_acme-challenge.int.example.net. IN CNAME  _acme-challenge.int.auth.example.net.
```
in the master zone file (which might be hosted outside AWS).

In the example above, certbot will be ask to request a certificate for int.example.net but the DNS-01 challenge should be created as  _acme-challenge.int.auth.example.net so in the Route53 managed sub-zone.

## Issue
At the moment, when running e.g.

`docker run -it --rm --name certbot -v "/root/route53awscredentials:/root/.aws/config" -v "/etc/letsencrypt:/etc/letsencrypt" -v "/var/lib/letsencrypt:/var/lib/letsencrypt" certbot/dns-route53 certonly --server https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory -n --agree-tos --email webmaster@example.net --dns-route53 -d int.example.net`

currently fail with
`PluginError: Unable to find a Route53 hosted zone for _acme-challenge.int.example.net`

As currently the certbot-dns-route53 plugin searches route53 for the requested domain(s)
[self._find_zone_id_for_domain(validation_domain_name)](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/590ec375ec89cce2a10e984c86fbf23d4533108f/certbot-dns-route53/certbot_dns_route53/dns_route53.py#L91)

## Solution
So the idea would be to to add a new argument to the certbot-dns-route53 to allow to pass the record name (either the domain name of the auth-subdomain) or the Route53 Zone-ID.

## References
See also [Allow _acme-challenge as a zone](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/5707)

issue_title: Proper python file encoding definition
issue_body:
All raiden python files seem to be using the [PEP 263](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0263/) encoding definition for UTF-8.

It's a minor request but the encoding is misspelt in all of the files. `utf8` is not the correct name. The encoding must always contain a dash, unless it's used in systems where dash is not acceptable. The correct name is `utf-8`. (source: [IANA](http://www.iana.org/assignments/character-sets/character-sets.xhtml))

So all 

```python
# -*- coding: utf8 -*-
```

should be replaced with

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
```

And since we are at it I would also suggest adding the python shebang on top there.
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
```


This is not just to be pedantic. Some editors (e.g.: emacs), don't recognize the requested encoding and need specific tweaks to work with the raiden sourcecode at the moment.

issue_title: Feature Request: Masking
issue_body:
(more requests from the trenches, as always love the work!)

I would love if torchtext handled masking for me. It's a small think, but I don't like that I have to pass around pad_index in my code. It breaks the abstraction. 

- Idea: batch object should have masks. Could we add something like this in addition to lengths or maybe just along with them?

```
def sequence_mask(lengths, max_len=None):
    """
    Creates a boolean mask from sequence lengths.
    """
    batch_size = lengths.numel()
    max_len = max_len or lengths.max()
    return (torch.arange(0, max_len)
            .type_as(lengths)
            .repeat(batch_size, 1)
            .lt(lengths.unsqueeze(1)))
```

- Another really useful mask is to blackout future words along with padding. We do something like this (although this code looks too complicated. 

```
    def _get_attn_subsequent_mask(self, size):
        ''' Get an attention mask to avoid using the subsequent info.'''
        attn_shape = (1, size, size)
        subsequent_mask = np.triu(np.ones(attn_shape), k=1).astype('uint8')
        subsequent_mask = torch.from_numpy(subsequent_mask)
        return subsequent_mask
```

issue_body:
### Actual behavior

Since a few days ago, the Travis run for Python 2.7 and PACKAGE_LEVEL=minimum fails.

The `make install` step installs the zhmcclient package itself, and that fails:

```
pip install -c minimum-constraints.txt .

        attrs = util.cfg_to_args(path, dist.script_args)

        cmdclass = ep.resolve()

Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /tmp/pip-tQyBiz-build/

It turns out that Travis uses Ubuntu 14.04 and Python 2.7.13.

The run on Python 2.7 and PACKAGE_LEVEL=latest succeeds.

When using Python 2.7.12 instead (and PACKAGE_LEVEL=minimum), it succeeds.

Looking at the log files (e.g. https://travis-ci.org/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/jobs/274588722), it turns out that `pip install setuptools===30.0.0` actually installs 36.0.1:

issue_title: [xray] Valgrind errors during fault scenario
issue_body:
This is on a branch, namely on 9fb8f1eec1d595c4f9a7d59db1aaf4610ce4d087 but I wanted to document them anyways, so we know how to reproduce it. Most likely the problem also happens on master and I'll test once the PRs 2582 and 2583 are in:

Then with valgrind activated (see PR 2582):

```
ray start --head --use-raylet
ray start --redis-address XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX:YYYYY --use-raylet --num-gpus 1
```

```
import ray

@ray.remote(num_gpus=1)
class Actor(object):
    def __init__(self):
        pass
    def f(self):
        pass

ray.init(redis_address="XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX:YYYYY", use_raylet=True)

a = Actor.remote()
ray.get(a.f.remote())
```

Then quickly (within <10s), kill the gpu raylet and run this:
```
ray.get(a.f.remote())
```

It will give the following

issue_title: Insufficient warning when trying to link an executable into another target
issue_body:
This is classified as an Error, but as it does not stop the build, it can go unnoticed.
What is the desired behaviour, should this simply stop the entire build or should it only stop the build after compiling everything that can be compiled?

Note that often, this is due to a target where the type was not specified and likely the auto-detection did not give the desired "header only" result. In this case, it should not be built as an executable and it would be better to stop everything before building.

issue_title: oidc auto refresh token during api invocation
issue_body:
Refresh the token not only at configuration initialization time but also during invocation of the K8 API calls. The token might expire right before a call thus causing the call to fail with an authentication failure. This will be needed for services that run a long time and rely on being able to reach the K8 Api Server (also short services that happen to be unlucky).

Possibly be done with a check to 'exp' field in the id-token right before a call_api or catch a 401 return status and perform a refresh token operation and update the configuration object with the new id_token and refresh_token values from the OAuth server.

Related to #368 #195, kubernetes-client/python-base#48

issue_title: Time Field in debug toolbar not updated on graphsql request
Respect content of REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE
issue_body:
It seems to me as if the gitlab package does not use the `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE` environment variable to do TLS verification:

```Python
gl = gitlab.Gitlab(remote_host_url, token, api_version=4)
gl.projects.get(id)
```
Fails:
```
(self, request, stream, timeout, verify, cert, proxies)
    504             if isinstance(e.reason, _SSLError):
    505                 # This branch is for urllib3 v1.22 and later.
--> 506                 raise SSLError(e, request=request)
    507 
    508             raise ConnectionError(e, request=request)

SSLError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='gitlab', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: //api/v4/projects/12 (Caused by SSLError(SSLError(1, u'[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:590)'),))
```

If I explicitly add the content of `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE` the connection works:
```Python
gl = gitlab.Gitlab(remote_host_url, token, api_version=4, ssl_verify=os.environ['REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE'])
```

Is this expected behavior? In my opinion the gitlab package should pass the content of `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE` to requests if it exists.

issue_title: Update Road Map
issue_body:
The Road Map on the README needs some work. What are our current long-term goals? For me it would be:

## DONE
1. Compiler support for most language elements.
2. Sympy back-end support for basic simulation and symbolic expression creation.
3. Casadi back-end DAE symbolic expression creation.

## TODO
1. Add Casadi based simulation support.
2. Handle when, if, and discrete time language elements in the compiler.
3. Add a method of serializing a flattened tree and reading it back from disk (possibly XML).
4. Support for higher dimensional arrays.
5. Support all of Modelica Standard Library
